They've survived with that one until now.
It'll be okay.
Hello.
Hi, Meenakshi.
Hi, how are you?
How are you?
All good.
Just waiting for the folks to join.
Okay.
You guys, hi.
Hello.
How are you?
All right.
So, busy week last week.
I was out in San Francisco, but a little drained from that.
Doing good otherwise.
You're so tired.
Yeah.
Yeah.
I need to catch up on sleep.
All right.
Should we get started, or should we?
Wait a little bit and see if...
Oh, there we go.
That's probably quorum now, right?
Manaswini is here.
Or do we think Rashid is going to join today or not?
Uh-huh.
I think that we can start.
I don't think Rashid will join, but we can start.
Ehtisham, you want to take it away?
Okay.
You
Ehtisham, you're on mute.
Okay, sorry.
We have submitted four more articles for review.
They are mentioned here on the sheet as well, and I've changed their status to ready to review in the sheet.
There are now a total of nine articles that are ready for review in the sheet, right?
So I have mentioned the 15 outlines here, even though we discussed the whole thing async on Slack, but I just mentioned it here for the record here.
So this is what we're going to do now, like bulk outline submission.
And then some of these articles, you'll notice that those were the ones that you move to outline needs to be revised or a similar status.

But we took your feedback, and then we took it to draft directly because, you know, if there's like two comments or three comments on an outline, we can easily just address it while taking two drafts.
So that's what we did.
We still be...
Still have like six more articles in the pipeline.
And so in the, like some today and some tomorrow, you'll see six more coming your way for review.
I've been tracking the GA4 data manually.
So there's some like this slight improvement in the, let me share.
I think I'm not sharing the right thing now.
We're doing something else here.
Okay, so there's slight improvement in the sessions and active users and new users and key events across the board for the 20 something articles that we have published for you.
So there's like the last time around from June 19th to July 16th was 1100 and 77 sessions.
Now it's 1248 and the date is also moving.
So it's not like we are overlapping by too much.
Let's go on.
All right, so, so.
All
Because we've done the analysis and the kind of topics we want to cover and everything before, I think today we should really just talk about production and production blockers from both sides.
So one of the concerns as the editor on this project that I have, I went through all the requirements, all the past feedback and everything.
What you are asking for, like it makes sense on paper, but I would go so far as to say it cannot be done at scale.
What you're looking for is articles that differentiate themselves from the top ranking four or five articles.
When we are targeting a keyword, we have to provide additional value and we have to describe why this article should exist as opposed to the top four or five articles.
That exists, that already rank for this keyword.
So in the past we did that.

Like not here at growthx, but a couple of years ago, I was working at animals and we used to do this for pretty much every article that we wrote and we wrote one and a half articles per week because of that, because you, this is something that AI, AI can just replicate what's already out there.
AI cannot really come up with new ideas, no matter what you do.
So, and I've, I've played around with it.
Like I also write stories with chat GPT and everything.
So I know that AI is like, it's, it can regurgitate what's already out there because it's trained on what's already out there.
So, whenever you want to go outside of the top four or five and you have to provide additional value, have to come up with new ideas.
The person actually working on the project, they have to go and do their research and read a bunch of stuff and then come up with that thing.
And that's a very time consuming thing.
So I'm not saying it cannot be done, but if you're looking for like 30 articles per month.
Then, you know, that's not going to happen if you're going to differentiate from the top ranking articles too much.

But if you're going to take the best information from the top ranking articles, and we are going to produce an article that has the best of the top four or top five ranking articles, then we can do this at scale and rapidly.
So that's like my biggest concern with the project at this point.

Yeah, I think just to echo that, like, that's probably why, you know, we ended up arriving at this disengagement, because I just think it's not a good fit in terms of like, what our product is designed for, because it's designed for scale of, you know, not terribly original, but very competent content, as opposed to like, high touch, very bespoke content.
Fair enough, I take your point, that's fair.
So our expectation was that

When we do the outline research, at this point, if you add like a point or two about, say, like a perspective that you want to take to the piece or a data point you found somewhere or an insight from some existing YouTube video that we might have, that could just serve as a differentiator, right?
That could bring perspective to the piece, which would make it stand out.
And that's something AI can execute.
If you give it an idea, then it can execute, right?
So that was our expectation right from the beginning.
We totally understand that this AI-generated content cannot come up with new ideas by itself.
But when it's mentioned in the outline, can 100% do that.
Monalisa?
Yeah, and to add to this point, we were actually doing this before, like David was also doing this.
And we have a bunch of content which were picked up from our YouTube channel, different photographers on the internet and quoting them.
So we were actually doing this previously.
No, no, I'm not talking about...
I'm talking about the answer to why this article should exist, how is it different from the top four or five articles.
To give it to AI, you have to do it manually first.
So you have to go in, you have to read four or five or six articles, then you have to come up with ideas.
That's what I'm talking about.
So that's a lot of manual work.
To come up with one paragraph and then you feed it to AI, then of course AI can do it.
Yeah, and even converting the original assets into it won't necessarily work.

Just having written 17,000, 18,000 articles myself personally by hand, like the, you know, we would always add original thing, but like it takes a lot to really differentiate into the way that it like dominates SERPs, right?
Like adding a, like piece of unique perspective is not enough to do that.
It takes the kind of work that Edesha was talking about, about like creating something net novel, like top to bottom.
Right.
Right.
So for example, in one of the articles that we've submitted, I think Manaswini, it was your feedback, you mentioned it in the outline that take a contrarian approach here, and so we took it.
So that's one way to differentiate, but even like if you want to go outside of that, and if you want to go contrarian or everything, that's not the word.

But if you want to go, if you want to really, really differentiate and come up with original points of view on articles, then it's actually better done manually because, you know, then you read, you do your homework, you come up with a point of view, then you kind of push that point of view throughout the article.

So, but that's, that's not, I'm not saying like it's all doom and gloom or something, we can still like make it work, but that was the biggest production like blogger on my side, because then I'm creating outline.
Then it will take a lot longer for me to create outlines.
cannot then do 15 outlines in a day.
Fair enough.
I understand.
Thank you so much for being super candid about this.
Appreciate it.
So the new set of keywords that I've shared, have you taken a look at that?
It's on the same tracking sheet.
I had mentioned it in the earlier outlines feedback message.
on Slack.
So there's a new set of keywords that we've queued for you.
Okay.
I'll use it.
This is a new tab in the AfterShoot sheet, right?
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah, the keywords tab.
I'm just going to open that sheet up to...
Yeah, with night, portrait photography, just a...
Yeah, so...
37 rows, right?
Yeah.
Yeah, so what they've done...
We are essentially is keep all of these very homogenous.
All of these are about different photography styles.
Right.
This is an attempt to make production easier for you guys because it almost flows programmatically.
All of these are similar keywords, similar intent.
This is like the perfect candidate for AI content.
So that's why we've queued it like that.
And further down the list, you see some templates or checklist items here, which I'm just going to go ahead and remove because this will be like separate effort for you guys for now.
Since we have a target to meet and we have a limited time, I'm just going to go ahead and queue a few more of the same kind of articles so that we can, you know, really fast track production.
Does this make sense, this approach?
Yeah, this makes sense, especially, I mean, I think I'm thinking about in the frame of the template that you sent over as well.
Is that the idea?
Yeah, that's meant to be.
That's this.
So this can follow the same set of rules and same set of logic.
And you.
AI-generated content becomes super easy because it has a very defined set of instructions.
That was the idea.
This is what we were working on.
This is why we had asked you to not focus on more keyword research so we can come up with a more homogenous set of stuff that becomes easy to produce with AI at scale.
So I hope this works.
And did you get a chance to go through that framework by any chance?
Yep.
Yeah, the one recent this morning.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
Yeah, yeah, did.
And I already ingested it into our generation tool.
So hopefully it will produce those results.
haven't tried actually running it yet, but it's all set up to go.
Awesome.
All right.
Let me know how that works.
If the framework was helpful or if you need any other detail, I've also actually added stuff about, you know, how do you pitch up to shoot at what points do you pitch?
What part of the product do you pitch and stuff like that?
Yeah, just so, you know, the AI can.
But yeah, let me know if you need anything else.
In the meantime, we'll just go ahead and queue a bunch of more keywords that are super homogenous and easy to produce so we can wrap things up quickly.
Okay.
Yeah, that sounds good.
And especially in that tab or in that document, the notes were also quite useful, right?
So if you have any more like that, it looks like maybe it was in progress and you have some other ones that you wanted to add, but that would be helpful too.
But the rest is very helpful already.
So thank you for sending that over.
Are you referring to the comments that we left in the aftershoot progress?
No, it's in the framework.
It's in the document that you shared, but then the last tab is the notes tab.
And I was like, oh, this is quite a useful resource.
But yeah.
Got it.
Got it.
Yeah, for sure.
Let me take a closer look at the new keywords as well and anything else.
that we might have queued for now from the list that we had before, and I'll be sure to add notes over there.
Okay, great.
Yeah, that's, I mean, that should be all we need to get back on track from a projection perspective.
But yeah, is there anything else you wanted to highlight or flag for us?
Oh, no, I think that's about it.
Yeah, Meenakshi, Monalisa, anything from your end?
No, none from my side.
Nothing.
We're good.
I think we've covered everything now.
Okay.
All right.
Well, thank you very much, and yeah, we'll get on this right away, and let's finish strong together, and hopefully we can both walk away somewhat satisfied.
Yeah.
Let me know if you have any blockers at all.
Super happy to help.
Okay.
Let's be in touch, I think.
Okay, thank you very much.
Thank you.
Thank you.
Take care.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync between AfterShoot and GrowthX to discuss content production progress, challenges, and strategy adjustments.](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Production challenges: Difficulty in creating highly differentiated content at scale (30 articles/month) while meeting AfterShoot's quality expectations](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=252.0)
  - [New keyword strategy: AfterShoot provided a homogenous set of 37 photography style keywords to streamline AI-driven content production](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=615.0)
  - [Framework update: GrowthX integrated AfterShoot's new content framework into their generation tool](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=751.0)
  - [Mutual goal: Finish the project strong despite earlier misalignments](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=850.0)

## Topics

### Content Production Challenges

  - [Ehtisham highlighted the difficulty in creating highly differentiated content at scale (30 articles/month)](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=240.0)
  - [AI limitations: Can replicate existing content but struggles with generating truly novel ideas](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=319.0)
  - [Time-consuming process to manually research and differentiate from top-ranking articles](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=345.0)
  - [Darrell confirmed this aligns with GrowthX's product design for competent but not highly bespoke content](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=387.0)
  - [Manaswini acknowledged the challenge, clarifying their expectation was for outline-driven differentiation](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)

### New Keyword Strategy

  - [AfterShoot provided 37 new, homogenous keywords focused on photography styles](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=615.0)
  - [Goal: Streamline AI content production by creating a more programmatic flow](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=667.0)
  - [Keywords are similar in intent, making them ideal for AI-generated content](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=674.0)
  - [Manaswini removed checklist/template items to focus on meeting targets within the limited timeframe](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=679.0)

### Content Framework Update

  - [AfterShoot shared a new content framework](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=766.0)
  - [Darrell confirmed it has been integrated into GrowthX's generation tool](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=751.0)
  - [Framework includes guidance on pitching AfterShoot and specific product features](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=766.0)
  - [Additional notes tab in the framework document provides useful resources](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=796.0)

### Project Wrap-up Strategy

  - [Both parties committed to finishing the project strong despite earlier misalignments](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=850.0)
  - [AfterShoot to queue more homogenous keywords for easier production](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=785.0)
  - [GrowthX to leverage the new framework and keywords to accelerate content creation](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=751.0)
  - [Open communication maintained for addressing any potential blockers](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=856.0)

## Next Steps

  - [GrowthX to begin content production using new homogenous keywords and framework](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=751.0)
  - [AfterShoot to review and add notes to the new keywords in the tracking sheet](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=835.0)
  - [AfterShoot to queue additional homogenous keywords to meet production targets](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=785.0)
  - [Both parties to maintain open communication about any production blockers](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=888.0)
  - [Schedule follow-up sync to assess progress and address any emerging issues](https://fathom.video/share/6YoTzzVN2mJhC-WRiKA64ZM4YL4fTYv7?tab=summary&timestamp=893.0)

action_items.description: Submit 6 more articles for review (some today, some tomorrow)
Remove template/checklist items from keyword list; queue more homogenous keywords for easier AI production
Test run new framework in AI generation tool with queued homogenous keywords
Add notes to new keywords in tracking sheet for additional context/guidance
